insight: Foundation's CommonCore.getOrDefault (CommonCore.java:1399) treats String values "" and "none" as absent and returns the default, but for all other types it is a plain null check, so Integer 0 or Boolean false pass through correctly. Relevant when adding Boss custom settings or config reads where 0/false are meaningful values, and when a String setting mysteriously falls back to default: the stored value may literally be "none".
